sketch0.title: Introduction to Text Concatenation in SQL

intro-concat.title: Introduction to Text Concatenation in SQL
intro-concat.bodyMarkdown:
Concatenation is the process of joining two or more text fields together into a single string. In SQL, you can use the `||` operator to concatenate text columns or literal strings. For example, to combine a customer's name and city, you might write:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ', ' || city AS name_and_city FROM customers;
```

This will produce a new column that joins the `full_name` and `city` columns, separated by a comma and space. If either value is `NULL`, the result will also be `NULL`.

concat-null-behavior.title: Handling NULLs in Concatenation
concat-null-behavior.bodyMarkdown:
When concatenating columns, if any value is `NULL`, the result of the concatenation will also be `NULL`. For example:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ' <' || email || '>' AS contact FROM customers;
```

If `email` is `NULL`, the entire `contact` value will be `NULL` for that row. To avoid this, you can use the `COALESCE()` function to substitute a default value for `NULL` fields:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ' <' || COALESCE(email, 'no-email') || '>' AS contact FROM customers;
```

concat-literals.title: Concatenating Literals and Columns
concat-literals.bodyMarkdown:
You can concatenate columns with literal strings to format your output. For example, to create a label for each customer:

```sql
SELECT 'Customer: ' || full_name AS label FROM customers;
```

This adds the literal text 'Customer: ' before each customer's name.

single-choice-concat-operator.title: Which operator is used to concatenate text in SQLite?
single-choice-concat-operator.prompt: You want to join two text columns in SQLite. Which operator should you use?

help.concept: In SQLite, the double pipe `||` is used to concatenate text values.
help.hint_1: Look for the operator that is specific to text concatenation in SQLite.
help.hint_2: The same operator is used in many SQL dialects for joining strings, and it looks like two vertical bars.

multi-choice-concat-results.title: What happens when concatenating columns with NULL values?
multi-choice-concat-results.prompt: Select all statements that are true when concatenating columns in SQLite where one or more values are NULL.
multi-choice-concat-results.hint: Think about how SQL treats NULLs in expressions.

help.concept: In SQLite, concatenating with NULL results in NULL. COALESCE() can be used to provide the correct answer fallback value.
help.hint_1: NULL values are not ignored in concatenation; they affect the result.
help.hint_2: If you want to avoid NULL results, you need to replace NULLs with something else before concatenating.

options.a: The result will be NULL if any value is NULL.
options.b: The result will be an empty string if any value is NULL.
options.c: You can use COALESCE() to replace NULLs before concatenation.

drag-reorder-concat-syntax.title: Order the parts to form a valid concatenation query
drag-reorder-concat-syntax.prompt: Arrange the tokens to create a SQL query that combines the full_name and city columns, separated by a comma and space.
drag-reorder-concat-syntax.hint: Start with the correct answer and end with a semicolon.

help.concept: A valid SQL concatenation query uses the `||` operator between columns and literals.
help.hint_1: The the correct answer clause comes first, and the correct answer comes before the table name.
help.hint_2: Remember to use `the correct answer` to give your result column a name.

fill-blank-choice-coalesce.title: Fill in the blank: Using COALESCE with concatenation
fill-blank-choice-coalesce.prompt:
Complete the query to show the full_name and email, but display 'no-email' if email is NULL:

```sql
SELECT full_name || ' <' || _____(email, 'no-email') || '>' AS contact FROM customers;
```
fill-blank-choice-coalesce.hint: Which function returns the first non-NULL value?

help.concept: the missing term() returns the first non-NULL argument, making it useful for replacing NULLs in concatenation.
help.hint_1: It's a standard SQL function for handling NULLs.
help.hint_2: This function can take multiple arguments and returns the first one that is not NULL.

fill-blank-choice-coalesce.template: SELECT full_name || ' <' || {blank} || '>' AS contact FROM customers;

code-input-concat-label.title: Write a query to create a customer label
code-input-concat-label.prompt: Write a SQL query that selects the id and a label column for each customer. The label should be formatted as 'Customer: ' followed by the full_name.
code-input-concat-label.hint: Use the || operator and a literal string.

help.concept: Concatenation joins text columns and literals using the `||` operator in SQLite.
help.hint_1: Start your SELECT with id and then concatenate the literal with the name.
help.hint_2: Remember to use AS to name your new column 'label'.
